rows.feature: POST /api/suggest — interactive drafting path (suggest count=3 -> S2 typed DraftSet)
rows.gap: Concrete interactive request/response JSON schema, count->takes mapping, and interactive-path timeout/retry/streaming + error taxonomy (429/502/empty) mapped onto DraftSet.

rows.feature: Client-supplied systemCore verbatim injection (the confirmed security hole / key-burn)
rows.gap: none for the drafting path — but see separate row: the same verbatim-systemCore wire is shared by 6 other call sites the plan does not migrate.

rows.feature: Shared /api/suggest wire reused by 6 non-drafting LLM call sites (judge, threadIntel, insight, dossier, ask, voicePersona)
rows.gap: A task-typed contract so the server can own/validate each of the 7 distinct systemCores (or a task id + structured-field schema per call site); otherwise the MUST-FIX either breaks the 6 or leaves their injection hole open.

rows.feature: judge (MessageJudge / pill 'Analyze' — score/works/risks/alternatives)
rows.gap: Whether judge migrates to structured output + server-owned core + server Safety, and its fallback/error behavior.

rows.feature: threadIntel (per-thread intel: urgency/action/question/commitments/tone/temp/effort/automated)
rows.gap: Decide which intel fields become deterministic FACTS vs LLM JUDGMENT; specify the enrichment-cache store (schema, durability, invalidation) named in Phase 5.

rows.feature: insight (legacy TOPIC/BRIEF single completion + deterministic-brief fallback)
rows.gap: Whether insight is retired or kept as fallback; if kept, its wire/contract under the new server-owned-core model.

rows.feature: dossier (person dossier single completion)
rows.gap: Endpoint/contract, entitlement gating, and error/fallback for dossier under the new backend.

rows.feature: ask (grounded free-form Q&A single completion)
rows.gap: Endpoint/contract, quota accounting (an ask also burns the key), and server Safety for the free-form ask path.

rows.feature: voicePersona (LLM read of the user's own voice)
rows.gap: Reconcile LLM voicePersona vs Phase-5 retrieval exemplars; specify whether voicePersona survives, its cache durability, and server-side Pro gating.

rows.feature: Server-enforced free-tier quota (checkAndConsume, 15 drafts / 7-day epoch bucket, 429)
rows.gap: Move the quota counter to durable Supabase (or equivalent) with a defined schema + week-bucket key, so redeploy/multi-instance can't reset or split it — Phase 0 names 'durable state' but does not specify this.

rows.feature: Client-side entitlement metering (Entitlements.decideDraft, 15/week, UX-only)
rows.gap: Define how server remaining (x-osmo-drafts-remaining/429) reconciles the client meter, and keep the two 15-values in lockstep.

rows.feature: Keyless mock fallback (mockTakes server-side + MockGenerator router fallback)
rows.gap: Specify mock/degraded-mode contract + labeling and whether keyless bypass of auth+quota is retained after the auth fix; define outage/cold-start UX (Phase 4 named-only).

rows.feature: Server-side Safety re-run (anti-manipulation guardrail on the server)
rows.gap: The Safety check itself is a trivially-bypassable keyword/co-occurrence floor (Safety.check); plan says re-run it server-side but does not specify hardening it or the server refusal response shape.

rows.feature: Client-side Safety pre-check (Safety.check before network)
rows.gap: none (subsumed by S3; the substantive weakness is the classifier quality, tracked in the server-Safety row).

rows.feature: Model selection / hardcoded claude-sonnet-5 + unvalidated model passthrough
rows.gap: Server-side model allowlist/pinning at /api/suggest (not just client registry) + the registry fetch/verify/fallback error path.

rows.feature: count parameter (accepted but never used) / N-takes generation
rows.gap: Explicitly retire/replace count and define min/expected takes enforced by the floor gate.

rows.feature: Autodraft (client AutodraftPolicy: Pro+human+needsReply, cap 30/day, suggest count=1, saves isAuto draft)
rows.gap: Server-side awareness/enforcement of autodraft (cap durability + quota accounting) and where the S4 background pipeline actually runs given the single always-on in-memory instance.

rows.feature: Background quality gate (S4 generate-2 -> Haiku judge -> S5 conditional repair)
rows.gap: A durable background-job substrate (queue, worker, idempotent result store) + its auth/quota accounting and failure handling — the plan names the stages but not the execution infrastructure.

rows.feature: Outcome logging / draft lineage / voice-exemplar promotion (S6 + LEARNING LOOP)
rows.gap: Concrete on-device exemplar+lineage schema, the retrieval index (Phase 5), the exact pseudonymous-scalar payload/endpoint that leaves the device, and the log-scrubbing enforcement/test.

rows.feature: Prompt-cache discipline (cache_control ephemeral on systemCore)
rows.gap: none — but the win depends on server-owned canonical core staying byte-stable across the 7 task cores; ensure the new structured-fields contract preserves the cached prefix.

rows.feature: Upstream error handling (non-2xx -> 502, no retry/backoff, no streaming)
rows.gap: Interactive-path error taxonomy (map Anthropic 429/500/529/timeout), bounded retry/backoff, and client timeout — none specified for the S2 interactive call.

rows.feature: RECLASSIFICATION of row 2 ('client-supplied systemCore verbatim injection => covered / none')
rows.gap: Row 2's 'covered / none for the drafting path' UNDERSTATES the coupling: the specified MUST-FIX (client sends structured fields; server owns the canonical systemCore) structurally breaks or bypasses the 6 co-hosted non-drafting call sites (row 3), so the drafting fix is NOT independently shippable and the key-burn hole stays open for the other 6 until a task-typed contract exists. It should be 'partial', not 'covered'.

rows.feature: GET /api/sync/pull (cursor pull from oplog)
rows.gap: Must add a durable per-device oplog store (Postgres/Supabase) with a restart-surviving, multi-instance-consistent monotonic seq, plus retention/GC. Plan only names 'durable state', never specifies pull's backing store or cursor durability.

rows.feature: POST /api/sync/send (blocking provider send, echo to oplog)
rows.gap: Must add a durable send-outbox with retry + an idempotency key (prevent double-send across restart), a durable provider-token store, and a specified 409/422/timeout contract. Plan only names OAuth-token durability.

rows.feature: GET /api/events (per-device SSE doorbell stream)
rows.gap: Must add cross-instance SSE fanout (Redis/Postgres pub-sub) or an explicit single-instance pin invariant, a reconnect/Last-Event-ID/redelivery contract, and address the ?token= query-auth surface. Plan only names 'durable state'.

rows.feature: Oplog store — per-device message log (pull substrate)
rows.gap: Must specify a durable per-device oplog table (seq PK, dedup index), a restart-surviving seq generator, retention/compaction, and multi-instance write serialization. Plan names the need but specifies none of it.

rows.feature: SSE fanout + publish() doorbell delivery (single-process)
rows.gap: Must add a pub/sub bus so doorbells fan out across instances (currently a doorbell published on instance A never reaches an SSE subscriber on instance B), and document the reconcile-fallback guarantee. Plan absent on realtime transport.

rows.feature: Cursor / seq semantics + idempotent re-pull dedup
rows.gap: Device-side idempotency is preserved by the plan, but the server monotonic per-device seq is not made durable/unique across restart+instances (today it resets to 0 on restart, which is only safe because re-register forces a full re-pull). Must specify a durable, gap-free, per-instance-safe seq source.

rows.feature: Client RealtimeSyncEngine + 401 restart-recovery contract
rows.gap: Once the server oplog is durable (Phase 0 goal), the 401->reset->full-re-pull contract becomes wrong/expensive; the plan must respecify device recovery so a durable backend does NOT force a cursor reset and full re-pull. Plan is silent on this device/server contract change.

rows.feature: Doorbell event schema (sync.dirty / connection.status / backfill.progress)
rows.gap: Plan does not mention the realtime event contract at all. Must preserve/version the doorbell schema (and the 'never send message bodies' invariant) under any new realtime transport.

rows.feature: E2EE multi-device DB sync (SyncEngine + BlobStore) — the whole cross-Mac sync backend
rows.gap: An entire cross-device sync backend (endpoint, per-user blob isolation/auth, durable cursors, conflict semantics, retention) is unbuilt and the plan NEVER mentions it. It also directly contradicts the PRIVACY CLAIM: with sync on, encrypted message blobs DO leave the Mac to Supabase/S3 — the plan's 'messages never leave your Mac' + log-scrubbing proof cover only the drafting proxy, not this sync egress.

rows.feature: Sync encryption: CryptoBox placeholder KDF + static shared salt + key/passphrase management
rows.gap: Production memory-hard KDF (Argon2id), per-user random salt, passphrase/keyphrase provenance, and key escrow/recovery/rotation are all unspecified. As written the 'E2EE' is a shared-salt passphrase derivation. Plan is silent on any of the sync-crypto key lifecycle.

rows.feature: POST /api/connect/link — mint OAuth/hosted-auth/mock URL + create pending link
rows.gap: Plan MUST specify a durable, multi-instance, single-use pending-link store (linkId + PKCE codeVerifier), and the connect-flow endpoint contract.

rows.feature: GET /api/oauth/google/callback — exchange code, store Gmail tokens, seed backfill
rows.gap: Plan MUST add durable encrypted token vault, refresh scheduling for the stored refresh_token, and state/CSRF hardening on the callback.

rows.feature: GET /api/oauth/slack/callback — exchange code, store Slack user token, seed DM backfill
rows.gap: Plan MUST add durable encrypted Slack-token storage, token refresh/re-auth handling, and callback state hardening.

rows.feature: GET /api/oauth/x/callback — PKCE exchange, store X tokens (~2h expiry), seed DM backfill
rows.gap: Plan MUST add durable token storage plus an actual refresh loop (wire refreshXToken) for the ~2h X tokens, or the connection is unusable after 2h / any restart.

rows.feature: OAuth token storage + refresh lifecycle (cross-cutting: setOAuthTokens/oauthTokens/refreshXToken)
rows.gap: Plan MUST specify: durable encrypted token store surviving restart/redeploy, a refresh scheduler/cron for all providers, invalid-token detection → re-auth prompt, and secret key rotation.

rows.feature: POST /api/connect/notify — Unipile hosted-auth callback (bind account_id to pending link)
rows.gap: Plan MUST add cryptographic (signature/HMAC) verification of the inbound callback, durable connection records, and idempotent binding.

rows.feature: POST /api/webhooks/unipile — inbound message_received / account.status webhook
rows.gap: Plan MUST add signature/HMAC verification of inbound webhooks, a durable/idempotent ingest log, and a multi-instance-safe fan-out (durable oplog + shared pub/sub).

rows.feature: Backfill engine — backfillConnection + gmail/slack/x backfills (fire-and-forget history import)
rows.gap: Plan MUST add a durable job queue + out-of-request worker, retry with backoff, resumable per-account cursor/checkpoint, and a durable oplog so imports survive restart/redeploy.

rows.feature: POST /api/connect/rebackfill — re-run history import for a connected account
rows.gap: Plan MUST fold this into a durable, idempotent, resumable backfill-job system (same as the main backfill engine gap).

rows.feature: POST /api/connect/mock/complete — mock-wizard 'Authorize' (dev/keyless connect completion)
rows.gap: Plan MUST define whether the keyless mock connect path persists (or is disabled in prod), and back it with the same durable link store.

rows.feature: GET /api/accounts — connections reconciliation snapshot (client rehydration source)
rows.gap: Plan MUST make connection records durable + multi-instance so the reconciliation snapshot survives restart; otherwise every redeploy shows all accounts disconnected.

rows.feature: PATCH /api/accounts?id= — pause/resume connection sync
rows.gap: Plan MUST persist connection sync-state (paused/connected) durably so a user's pause survives redeploy.

rows.feature: DELETE /api/accounts?id= — disconnect connection
rows.gap: Plan MUST specify durable disconnect (tombstone), provider token revocation, and ensure delete is not undone by restart/adoption.

rows.feature: adoptOrphanedAccounts — single-tenant restart-heal (OSMO_ADOPT_ORPHANS)
rows.gap: Plan MUST replace this single-tenant env-gated hack with durable per-device connection records, making adoption obsolete (Phase 0 durable state should cover connections but does not).

rows.feature: Client ConnectionsManager reconcile — phases; never rehydrates 'connected' phase from disk
rows.gap: Plan MUST specify durable server connection records AND client-side connected-phase persistence so reconcile distinguishes 'server forgot' from 'truly disconnected' across restarts.

rows.feature: POST /api/device/register — keyless device credential minting
rows.gap: Rate-limit/abuse control on unlimited keyless device minting (a direct key-burn/quota-abuse vector), plus durable+multi-instance device-token storage so 'device auth' survives restart. Phase 0 names durability but leaves both unspecified.

rows.feature: requireDevice — device Bearer token auth (in-memory + ?token= fallback)
rows.gap: Durable/multi-instance token store (tokens currently invalidated on every restart/redeploy), removal of the ?token= querystring fallback, and token expiry/rotation. Plan names device auth but specifies none of these.

rows.feature: POST /api/license/validate — license-key validation (MOCK: any OSMO- prefix = Pro)
rows.gap: A real license-key validation backend (Stripe subscription / license server) to replace the mock 'any OSMO-…=active Pro'. The plan's phases never mention licensing at all.

rows.feature: POST /api/license/reset — dev/keyless subscription+trial reset
rows.gap: Plan does not address it. Self-disables when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is present, so low priority, but a production plan should confirm it is gated/removed in live mode.

rows.feature: POST /api/trial/start — 14-day server-recorded trial (idempotent, durable)
rows.gap: Trial-abuse prevention: trial is keyed to deviceId, but device minting is open+unlimited, so re-registering yields infinite fresh trials. The plan's durable-state phase never addresses trial identity/abuse.

rows.feature: POST /api/promo/redeem — promo/referral codes (hardcoded FRIEND/LAUNCH/WELCOME)
rows.gap: A real promo/referral table with single-use enforcement and redemption tracking to replace the in-code hardcoded catalog. Plan does not mention promos.

rows.feature: POST /api/account/link — Sign in with Apple (appleUserID TRUSTED, no verification)
rows.gap: Server-side Apple identity-token (JWT) verification. appleUserID is trusted as sent, so any client can claim any Apple ID and merge/steal that account's subscription — a real auth hole the plan's security scope (suggest route only) never touches.

rows.feature: POST /api/account/upgrade — web-session 'go Pro' (mock activates, no payment)
rows.gap: Real Stripe Checkout wiring (currently a 501 TODO in live mode, and mock activates Pro with no charge). Plan does not address billing/upgrade.

rows.feature: POST /api/account/delete — durable purge of device+account records
rows.gap: The plan's privacy claim covers not retaining message content during drafting, not account/data deletion. The durable-state phase never specs a deletion/GDPR-purge contract for licensing+account rows.

rows.feature: Entitlement signing — Ed25519 signed entitlement (committed DEV keypair fallback)
rows.gap: Production entitlement-signing key management: remove the COMMITTED DEV private key fallback (repo access = forge Pro entitlements), add key rotation and app/server public-key match verification. Phase 2 introduces a SEPARATE Ed25519-signed CONFIG registry but never addresses the entitlement signer.

rows.feature: Durable accounts store + subscription resolution (device→user→sub; Supabase users/devices/subscriptions)
rows.gap: Phase 0 names durability but leaves subscription resolution, multi-instance safety, and the in-memory-vs-durable split unspecified — notably the Stripe webhook writes subscription state to the EPHEMERAL memoryStore, not this durable store (see webhook row).

rows.feature: POST /api/checkout/session — mint Stripe/mock checkout URL
rows.gap: Real Stripe Checkout Session creation (live path is an unimplemented TODO that falls through to mock). Plan does not address billing/checkout.

rows.feature: GET /api/checkout/mock-complete — dev mock purchase activation
rows.gap: Plan does not address it. Self-disables when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is set, so low priority; production plan should confirm it stays gated.

rows.feature: POST /api/webhooks/stripe — Stripe subscription lifecycle → license state (scaffold)
rows.gap: Real Stripe signature verification + event handling AND writing to the DURABLE accounts store. Today the webhook writes to the EPHEMERAL in-memory memoryStore (getStore().setLicense), so webhook-activated subscriptions are wiped on restart AND are invisible to /api/license/validate (which reads the accounts store) — a live durability/consistency bug the plan never addresses.

rows.feature: Per-account (not per-device) quota + trial rollup
rows.gap: Quota and trial must aggregate at the user/account level (device→user rollup) or open device minting resets both. Rows 10 (durability) and 49 (trial abuse) cover adjacent pieces but not the core keying flaw: cost/entitlement is metered per-device, not per-account, so identity multiplication defeats it. Also the Swift client meter (15/week) and server meter must be reconciled per-account.

rows.feature: POST /api/checkout/session — mint app checkout URL / create real Stripe Checkout Session
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: real stripe.checkout.sessions.create with subscription mode, a plan→price-ID map, success/cancel URLs, and remove the current TODO fall-through that returns a mock URL even in Stripe mode (so 'stripe-pending' never actually charges).

rows.feature: GET /api/checkout/mock-complete — mock purchase completion that grants Pro
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: removal or hard build-time gating of this no-auth Pro-grant path (currently the entire billing bypass while launching keyless/free-first), or bind it to an authenticated dev-only credential.

rows.feature: POST /api/account/upgrade — web-session 'go Pro' from the account portal
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: a real web Stripe Checkout Session (client_reference_id=user.id, subscription mode) returning a redirect URL, replacing the 501 stub and the mock grant.

rows.feature: POST /api/webhooks/stripe — Stripe subscription-lifecycle webhook → license state
rows.gap: Production plan MUST specify: stripe.webhooks.constructEvent signature verification, the Stripe Node SDK dependency, routing writes to the DURABLE accounts store (reconciling the read/write disconnect), event idempotency/dedupe, and full lifecycle mapping (created/updated/deleted, past_due, canceled).

rows.feature: Subscription state storage & reconciliation (webhook-write vs read-path disconnect)
rows.gap: Production plan MUST declare the durable accounts store (osmo_subscriptions) the single source of truth for billing and route ALL payment writes there; the current webhook path to memoryStore.licenses is lost on restart and invisible to /api/license/validate.

rows.feature: POST /api/license/validate — entitlement source of truth + license-key redemption
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: a real license/subscription lookup (replacing the mock 'OSMO-' acceptance) and specify the entitlement-signing + offline-grace contract, keeping it separate from the config registry signing.

rows.feature: POST /api/license/reset — dev/keyless-only subscription+trial reset
rows.gap: Low-severity: a dev-only test endpoint the plan does not enumerate; a production plan should at minimum confirm it stays disabled in live mode. Not covered.

rows.feature: POST /api/trial/start — start server-recorded 14-day trial per device
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add trial lifecycle (start/expiry/tier gating, abuse prevention on re-register) — the plan is entirely about drafting and never covers monetization trials.

rows.feature: POST /api/promo/redeem — promo / referral codes (trial-extend + discount ack)
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: a real promo/referral store (replacing the hardcoded FRIEND/LAUNCH/WELCOME catalog) and actual application of discount codes as Stripe coupons at checkout.

rows.feature: Payment-processor integration infrastructure (Stripe SDK, price catalog, customer portal, cancellation/refund/dunning)
rows.gap: Production plan MUST add: the Stripe Node SDK dependency, Stripe env/secrets wiring in render.yaml, a plan→price-ID catalog, customer-portal (self-serve cancel/manage), and cancellation/refund/dunning → entitlement flows. Entire payment-processor layer is unspecified.

rows.feature: GET /api/media (binary attachment proxy: device-token auth + per-platform connection-ownership gate)
rows.gap: Plan must add the /api/media endpoint contract: params (platform/messageRef/attachmentRef/mime), the 'device must own a connection for this platform' precondition, and 400/401 semantics. Auth mechanism aligns with Phase 0 but is not written for this route.

rows.feature: Gmail attachment fetch (Gmail API v1 users/me/messages/*/attachments via stored OAuth access_token)
rows.gap: Plan must specify Gmail media proxying: a durable/refreshable Google token source (today in-memory, wiped on restart), 502/404 handling, and survival across redeploy. Phase 0 only NAMES 'OAuth tokens' as durable state with no mechanism.

rows.feature: Slack attachment fetch (direct url_private fetch + SSRF hostname allowlist files.slack.com)
rows.gap: Plan must specify Slack media proxying incl. the SSRF allowlist (files.slack.com), stored Slack-token durability, streamed-body passthrough, and the 400/502/413 error contract. Absent from the drafting-focused plan.

rows.feature: Unipile attachment fetch (downloadAttachment for linkedin/whatsapp/instagram; live-key gate, mock→null→placeholder)
rows.gap: Plan must specify Unipile media proxying: isLiveUnipile() live-vs-mock gating, connection-id ownership, 404 on missing bytes, and tenant-key handling. Not mentioned.

rows.feature: No-error placeholder fallback (no connection / no token / mock → 1x1 transparent PNG, cache-control no-store)
rows.gap: Plan must preserve/define the 'always render something' contract: after restart wipes in-memory OAuth tokens, media degrades to a placeholder, not an error. A durable-token redesign changes this behavior and must state it explicitly.

rows.feature: 25MB size cap → 413 (byteLength for Gmail/Unipile; content-length for Slack)
rows.gap: Plan must define an attachment size limit / 413 policy (today 25MB, enforced post-buffer for Gmail/Unipile and via content-length header for Slack). Absent.

rows.feature: Response caching (Cache-Control: private, max-age=86400) with no server/CDN media cache
rows.gap: Plan must define a media caching/CDN strategy. Today only a per-response private max-age=86400 header; no durable server cache. A multi-instance/durable design must decide where (and whether) cached bytes live server-side.

rows.feature: In-memory OAuth-token + connection dependency (media breaks after restart/redeploy until user reconnects)
rows.gap: Plan must specify how OAuth tokens + connections become durable/multi-instance so media survives redeploy. This is the exact 'named but not specified' Phase-0 hole called out in the scope note; today all state is in-memory Maps.

rows.feature: Client MediaStore on-disk cache (never evicted, v1) + ensureMediaFetched → fetchMedia (offline/retry behavior)
rows.gap: Plan must address client media lifecycle: unbounded never-evicted on-disk cache (no eviction policy), single-retry-then-silent-fail fetch, and no offline queue. Plan's learning loop covers drafts/voice exemplars, not media bytes.

rows.feature: Client avatar fetch from arbitrary wire URLs (SSRF/privacy egress, no proxy, no durable cache)
rows.gap: Plan's media + privacy story omits avatars entirely: no server proxy/allowlist for avatar bytes, no scheme/host validation, no durable avatar cache, no reconciliation with the media-durability redesign. Avatar bytes also become part of the E2EE blob egress once synced.

rows.feature: POST /api/auth/request — mint 15-min single-use magic link (unauthenticated, {email})
rows.gap: Production plan must specify the magic-link mint endpoint: single-use token + 15-min expiry model, and real email delivery so the verify URL is NOT returned in the response body.

rows.feature: Magic-link email delivery (Resend) — currently a TODO; verifyUrl leaked in response body even when RESEND_API_KEY set
rows.gap: Resend integration is unbuilt and verifyUrl is returned in the JSON body even in 'sent' mode (magic-link leak = account takeover for any email). Plan's Phase 0 'Security' is scoped solely to the /api/suggest systemCore/Safety fix and never closes this hole.

rows.feature: Abuse / rate limiting on unauthenticated auth endpoints (/api/auth/request)
rows.gap: No rate limiting / anti-abuse on the open POST /api/auth/request → enables email-bombing and magic-link enumeration. Plan defines no throttling for auth endpoints.

rows.feature: GET /api/auth/verify — consume token, find-or-create user (first login = signup), mint web session, 303 → /account
rows.gap: Plan must specify the consume/verify flow: single-use token consumption + 15-min expiry check, first-login=signup find-or-create semantics, session creation, and expired/invalid-token redirect handling.

rows.feature: POST /api/auth/logout — delete server session + clear cookie
rows.gap: Plan never addresses session teardown: server-side session-row deletion, cookie clearing, or CSRF protection on this cookie-auth POST.

rows.feature: Web session lifecycle & cookie security (osmo_session httpOnly SameSite=Lax 30-day)
rows.gap: Plan specifies nothing for the web session model: 30-day sliding cookie with NO server-side expiry/rotation, no CSRF on cookie-auth POSTs (logout/upgrade), no cookie signing/secret rotation, and no multi-instance validation (the in-memory fallback session map is per-instance).

rows.feature: POST /api/account/delete — device-auth purge of in-memory state + cascade delete of user/devices/sub/sessions (data deletion)
rows.gap: The privacy claim ('keep none') and Phase 0/5 add new durable/cached server state (durable oplog, OAuth tokens, quota, enrichment cache, exemplar lineage) that a deletion path is obligated to purge — yet the plan never extends /api/account/delete to cover them, nor specifies idempotency, two-store partial-failure handling, or deletion verification/audit.

rows.feature: /account page — server component, cookie-auth, renders identity + plan/tier + linked Macs, redirects to /login when unauthenticated
rows.gap: Plan doesn't address the authenticated web account view: tier resolution/display, linked-device listing, or the cookie-gated redirect flow.

rows.feature: /login page — email form, calls /api/auth/request, shows dev-mode magic link inline
rows.gap: Plan doesn't address the login UI, and in dev mode it renders the verify URL inline (mirrors the response-body link leak) — no plan guidance on gating that.

rows.feature: /download page — 'Coming soon' disabled button, NO actual download/notarized-build link
rows.gap: Download page is a stub (disabled 'Coming soon', no notarized build URL). Plan (drafting/LLM-only) never wires release-artifact distribution/versioning for the Mac app.

rows.feature: Durable accounts/session data model (Supabase osmo_users/devices/subscriptions/magic_links/web_sessions + in-memory fallback selector)
rows.gap: Phase 0's durable-state goal names durability generically but says nothing about the accounts/session/magic-link tables. The in-memory fallback store (MemoryAccountsStore, used when Supabase keys are absent) is per-instance and wiped on restart, and multi-instance session/magic-link validation is left unspecified — a gap the plan's durability objective names but does not close for this subsystem.

rows.feature: User data export / portability (GDPR/CCPA access right)
rows.gap: GDPR/CCPA require data ACCESS/EXPORT, not just deletion. No export/portability path exists anywhere, and the plan (like rows 53/84 for deletion) never specs export across the durable + net-new state. Deletion completeness (row 84) and export are both obligations the durable-state phases create but do not discharge.

rows.feature: POST /api/enrich/person endpoint (orchestration contract)
rows.gap: Production plan must specify the enrich endpoint itself: request/response contract, device-token auth, result-size caps, and its place relative to the drafting pipeline. Today it fans out to TWO paid upstreams (Unipile+Exa) the plan never acknowledges.

rows.feature: Per-device rate limiting (10 req / 60s sliding window)
rows.gap: This window is the SOLE cost control on two paid upstreams (no server cache exists). It is a per-process Map that resets on redeploy and diverges across instances. Plan Phase 0 only NAMES durable/multi-instance state (specifies just the systemCore/Safety/auth fix); it must add durable/shared rate accounting or the limiter is defeated by any restart or second instance.

rows.feature: LinkedIn profile fetch via Unipile (getUserProfile)
rows.gap: Plan ignores the LinkedIn enrichment path entirely: no mention of Unipile, of the in-memory connections store's durability (wiped on redeploy → enrichment silently loses LinkedIn until re-connect), or of OAuth/account-token persistence. Must specify connection-state durability and the degradation contract.

rows.feature: Exa public-web search + retry/backoff
rows.gap: Plan is silent on the web-search upstream: no Exa key handling, retry/backoff policy, query construction, result-size caps, or cost governance. The signed registry (Phase 2) is scoped to LLM directives/thresholds/model-ids and does not cover this non-LLM upstream.

rows.feature: Keyless deterministic mock enrichment mode
rows.gap: Plan never mentions keyless mock enrichment. Golden-set eval (Phase 3) and cold-start/outage handling (Phase 4) are drafting-only; plan should state whether mock enrichment persists and how it interacts with eval/demo determinism.

rows.feature: Source-resolution degradation ladder + 502 outage semantics
rows.gap: Plan defines no error taxonomy for enrichment. The distinction between real-outage (502) and legitimate-emptiness (source=none) is load-bearing for the client and must be specified; today it lives only in code.

rows.feature: Client-side PersonEnrichment cache (device-local DB, 7-day staleness, refetch)
rows.gap: Plan never mentions the on-device enrichment cache or its 7-day refresh policy. It must be reconciled with the proposed server-side 'enrichment cache' (Phase 5): key coherence, which side owns TTL, and avoiding double-caching / stale divergence.

rows.feature: Consent gating (enrichmentEnabled toggle + remote flag 'enrichment')
rows.gap: Phase 2 registry can host the 'enrichment' flag, but the plan (a) doesn't specify it, (b) ignores the local enrichmentEnabled consent toggle, and (c) its PRIVACY CLAIM is scoped to drafting ('to draft we send Claude the thread...') and does NOT cover name/handle/hints egress to Unipile+Exa. Plan must extend the consent + log-scrub/privacy story to the enrichment egress surface.

rows.feature: Server-side enrichment cache (Phase 5 proposal)
rows.gap: Phase 5 names an 'enrichment cache' and specifies nothing: cache key (name+linkedinHandle+hints?), backing store (durable vs the current all-in-memory design that a redeploy wipes), TTL vs the client's 7 days, invalidation, per-device isolation/privacy, and size caps. This is net-new server state and is under-specified.

rows.feature: Client offline/retry behavior (keep-last, empty-this-session, force toast)
rows.gap: Plan says nothing about enrichment offline/retry semantics. If a durable server cache is added (Phase 5), the interaction with the client's keep-last-on-failure and empty-this-session guards must be defined so stale/empty states stay coherent.

rows.feature: GET /api/config/flags — remote feature flags + kill-switch (aiDrafting/autodraft/enrichment/media/webhooks)
rows.gap: Must specify SERVER-SIDE kill-switch enforcement: today /api/suggest never checks aiDrafting (client-enforced only), so a registry flag is not a real kill switch — a keyless bearer still burns the Anthropic key. Also missing: registry endpoint shape, durable storage, fetch auth, and signature-failure/offline fallback flag values.

rows.feature: GET /api/health — uptime probe + incident-banner broadcast (OSMO_STATUS / OSMO_STATUS_MESSAGE)
rows.gap: Plan must define a health/incident-banner mechanism (or fold the OSMO_STATUS broadcast into the signed registry) and specify per-instance liveness/readiness once multi-instance is on the table — currently entirely unaddressed.

rows.feature: GET /api/version — update-check stub (hardcoded 0.2.0 / build 2 / downloadURL / notes)
rows.gap: Plan must own version/update-check delivery: the payload is hardcoded and must be edited+redeployed for every release (contradicting the 'iterate without OTA' goal). Registry could carry it but plan explicitly scopes registry to directives/thresholds/model ids/flags only.

rows.feature: POST /api/feedback — in-app feedback / bug reports (device-auth)
rows.gap: Plan must specify durable storage + routing for user feedback/bug reports (currently ephemeral, lost on restart/redeploy) and reconcile with the privacy claim, since free-text feedback may contain message content that 'never leaves the Mac' promises exclude.

rows.feature: POST /api/dev/emit — E2E trigger to inject a scripted inbound message (mock-mode only, device-auth)
rows.gap: Phase 0 (security) must explicitly env-gate or remove dev routes in prod: today the ONLY gate is mock-mode, so a keyless prod deploy (no UNIPILE_DSN/API_KEY ⇒ mock mode) exposes emit, letting ANY registered device inject arbitrary inbound messages.

rows.feature: GET /api/dev/outbox — E2E assertion surface returning recorded /api/sync/send outbox (mock-mode only, device-auth)
rows.gap: Same as dev/emit: Phase 0 must env-gate/remove dev routes in prod — mock-mode is the only gate, so a keyless prod deploy exposes another device's-scoped outbox to any registered device; also an information surface the security fix ignores.

rows.feature: instrumentation.ts register() — boot-time Unipile webhook registration (once per server process)
rows.gap: Phase 0 NAMES durable/multi-instance state but does not specify boot-time webhook registration under multi-instance: the 'ensured' latch is per-process and the design assumes one target URL / one instance, so N instances would each reconcile the same tenant-wide hooks — a multi-instance ops behavior the plan leaves unspecified.

rows.feature: Local SQLCipher DB-key management + backup/restore/disaster recovery
rows.gap: No backup/restore/export of the local encrypted DB, no key escrow/recovery, and no backup/PITR plan for the durable Supabase or the to-be-durable oplog/tokens. Phase 0 'durable state' names durability but never specs backup/DR for either the on-device DB or server stores.

rows.feature: Sparkle OTA appcast feed + EdDSA update-signing key + notarized-DMG distribution (release↔version coupling)
rows.gap: The OTA delivery backend (appcast hosting, update-signing key mgmt/rotation, DMG distribution, /download wiring) is a separate system the drafting-only plan ignores. Security-critical psychology-core changes STILL require an OTA (the registry's 'iterate without OTA' does not cover the app-bundled core or signer keys), and the backend-registry ↔ app-version compatibility contract is unspecified. Rows 87 (/download stub) and 101 (/api/version stub) touch only fragments.

rows.feature: Golden-set eval harness infrastructure (Phase 3)
rows.gap: Phase 3 NAMES the golden set but specifies no capsule data model/store, eval runner, scoring rubric, CI/regression-gating, or execution location; it is net-new infra with no design, and no harness exists in the repo. Without it the S4 Haiku-judge/quality claims are unverifiable.

rows.feature: Signed config registry: its OWN signing-key lifecycle + client fetch/verify/cache/offline path
rows.gap: Beyond row 99's endpoint/storage/flag-enforcement points, the registry's own signing-key management and the client bundle/verify/cache/rotation path are unspecified. Two independent Ed25519 key domains (entitlement + registry) now need coordinated lifecycle management the plan never lays out.

rows.feature: Observability: logging / metrics / alerting / crash-restart & error detection
rows.gap: A single always-on in-memory instance where restart WIPES all state has NO alerting on restart/crash, error-rate, Anthropic spend, quota exhaustion, SSE subscriber count/leaks, provider-webhook failures, or backfill failures; no metrics/tracing/log aggregation. The plan (drafting-only) never specs observability, which is a prerequisite to even notice the state-loss events it depends on.

rows.feature: Global rate-limiting / abuse / DoS protection across ALL endpoints
rows.gap: There is no cross-cutting rate-limiting/abuse layer. The single instance is a trivial DoS + Anthropic-key-burn target; unauthenticated register/auth endpoints are unthrottled (enabling email-bomb, device-mint, quota-reset abuse). The plan defines no shared throttling substrate; the enrich limiter (the only one) is itself defeated by any restart or second instance.

rows.feature: Global Anthropic spend cap / budget circuit-breaker
rows.gap: Only per-device 15-drafts/week (itself resettable via open device minting + fresh trials) guards cost. There is no global daily/monthly Anthropic budget cap, spend alarm, or automatic degrade-to-mock on breach. Even with per-user quota enforced, trial abuse or a keyless bypass can run the bill unbounded; the plan has no cost-governance ceiling.

rows.feature: Deploy/rollout strategy: state-loss on every redeploy, no graceful drain / migrations / instance-count guard
rows.gap: No blue-green / connection-draining / canary, no DB-migration ordering for the Phase-0 durable stores, and no ENFORCED single-instance invariant (a manual scale-up silently corrupts, per the system note). The plan names durability but never specs the rollout, migration, or scale-guard needed to actually cut over from the wipe-on-deploy design.

rows.feature: Secrets rotation & key-compromise runbook (Anthropic key, OAuth client secrets, entitlement signer, webhook secret)
rows.gap: A holistic secret-rotation + compromise-response plan (Anthropic key, OAuth client secrets, webhook secret, both Ed25519 signers) is unspecified. The entitlement-key/app-bundle coupling means signer rotation must be coordinated with an app release. Rows 34/54 touch only the OAuth-token-encryption key and the entitlement key narrowly.

rows.feature: Identity-graph rebuild on sync + merge-decision durability / cross-device coherence
rows.gap: Plan never addresses where merge decisions (rejectedPairKeys, confirmed merges) are stored, their durability, or cross-device consistency: once the E2EE DB sync is on, two Macs independently re-cluster and could resurrect a rejected merge or diverge on personID. The identity-graph state, its sync, and merge-decision reconciliation are unspecified.

rows.feature: Anthropic data-retention / ZDR / DPA dependency behind the 'keep none, never train' privacy claim
rows.gap: The log-scrubbing test proves OSMO's OWN proxy doesn't retain, but the 'keep none / never train' promise also depends on Anthropic's upstream retention (default 30-day) and requires a Zero-Data-Retention agreement + DPA + sub-processor disclosure. The plan's privacy proof is scoped to OSMO's logs only and never addresses the processor terms that the public claim actually rides on.
